title: Scandix claytonii
taxonomy: Scandix claytonii is a species of plant in the family Apiaceae. The classification of Scandix claytonii is within the genus Scandix. It is commonly known as Clayton's shepherds needle or Clayton's fennel-flower. Scandix claytonii is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, sandy soils. Its classification is also sometimes referred to as a synonym of Scandix pecten-veneris.
uses: Scandix claytonii is also known as Shepherd's-needle or Needle-flower. Scandix claytonii has been traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant's seeds and roots are used in various traditional remedies, particularly in Europe and North Africa. The plant contains compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses.
